pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: NAOV surged 46.6% on 2025-03-17 opening (38.7 vs 26.4 prior close) but reversed -43.9% over 5 days. Surge coincides with 1:11 reverse split on 2025-03-14 (mechanical, not value-creating). Critical structural absences prevent high-confidence short thesis: (1) SEC filing confirmation failed (cik_not_found), preventing verification of any official disclosure; (2) No news items in 7-day window despite 20 items dropped post-cutoff, suggesting narrative event occurred but is unverifiable in packet; (3) Fundamental data insufficient (no PIT-safe quarterly statements); (4) Valuation deeply distressed (PE=-0.077, net_margin=-583.74%, PB=0.034); (5) Alternative data adapters failed or returned no rows (GitHub unmapped, Form 4 cik_not_found, 13F unavailable); (6) Analyst sentiment stale (Nov 2024, 1 buy). Under asymmetric absence rule, structural absence of SEC filings for a listed equity claiming a material event is bear-thesis evidence. However, the absence of hard-to-fake corroboration (SEC filings, insider activity, recent news, developer footprint) combined with the unverifiable narrative (20 items dropped post-cutoff) prevents establishing a defensible short thesis. The -43.9% reversal suggests momentum rather than value creation, but without confirmation of the catalyst or recent fundamentals, conviction is insufficient. Upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verify, Surge-Short sleeve) all recommend 'watch' or 'needs_more_evidence' due to sparse corroboration. Risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Macro regime is restrictive (equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation_caution), which argues against micro-cap shorts. Preponderance of evidence does not support entry at this time.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K or press release confirming a material acquisition, partnership, or product launch is later retrieved and verified, the structural-absence bear thesis weakens.
If quarterly fundamentals show positive revenue growth and path to profitability, the distressed valuation may be justified as a turnaround.
If institutional ownership data (13F) shows significant new positions by reputable funds, the absence of corroboration may be a data lag rather than a signal.
If the reverse split is part of a legitimate recapitalization or uplisting plan with disclosed milestones, the momentum may reflect real value creation.

suggested_improvements: Retrieve SEC EDGAR filings directly (bypass FMP adapter) to confirm CIK and check for 8-K, 10-Q, or press releases filed on or before 2025-03-17.
Cross-check news sources (Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance, company IR) for any catalyst narrative published in the 7-day window.
Obtain quarterly fundamentals from SEC filings (10-Q) to assess revenue, profitability, and cash burn.
Verify the reverse split's purpose and any associated corporate action (recapitalization, uplisting, debt restructuring).
Check insider Form 4 filings (SEC EDGAR direct) for any officer/director transactions around the surge date.
Assess whether NAOV is a shell company, SPAC, or distressed micro-cap with limited disclosure obligations.
Implement post-cutoff news adapter with explicit 'news_broke_after_cutoff' flag to disambiguate whether the catalyst is real but post-decision or absent entirely.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The packet does not include the company's sector classification (fundamental_snapshot.sector is absent), preventing sector-specific evidence assessment per the SECTOR-AWARE REASONING SEQUENCE. The news_event_summary block shows 20 items dropped post-cutoff but does not include the trigger_article or catalyst_type, making it impossible to identify the specific narrative driving the surge. The filing_confirmation block failed entirely (cik_not_found), which is a hard blocker for a listed equity. The alternative_data_features block is largely a placeholder (adapters not wired in v1), limiting corroboration. The narrative_price_gap_assessment is marked not_evaluated pending agent_LLM_reasoning, which is not available in this packet.
recommended_followups: Retrieve NAOV's CIK directly from SEC EDGAR and pull all filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1) filed on or before 2025-03-17 to identify the catalyst.
Search public news archives (Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance, PR Newswire) for NAOV announcements in the 7-day window.
Obtain the most recent 10-Q or 10-K to assess revenue, profitability, and cash position.
Verify the reverse split's purpose and any associated corporate action or recapitalization plan.
Check insider Form 4 filings for any officer/director transactions or stock sales around the surge date.
Assess whether NAOV is a shell company, SPAC, or distressed micro-cap with limited disclosure obligations.
Monitor for SEC filing confirmation and fundamental data verification before escalating to 'short' decision.
pm_decision_extract.reason: Insufficient evidence to establish defensible short thesis. Surge appears driven by reverse split (mechanical, not value-creating) and unverified narrative (20 news items dropped post-cutoff, no SEC filing confirmation). Company is deeply distressed (PE=-0.077, net_margin=-583.74%), but without access to recent fundamentals or official disclosures, bear thesis cannot be cleanly supported. Structural absence of SEC filings, insider activity, and recent news is bear-thesis evidence, but absence of hard-to-fake corroboration prevents high-confidence entry. Recommend monitoring for SEC filing confirmation and fundamental data before entering position.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: NAOV exhibits a severe 5-day drawdown of -43.91% with relative volume 5.83x the 20-day average, triggering on 2025-03-17 at market open (trigger_day_open=38.7, last_eod_close=22.1). A 1:11 stock split occurred on 2025-03-14 (3 trading days prior), which mechanically explains the price discontinuity but does NOT explain the magnitude of the volume surge or the intraday collapse. The news_event_summary block is status=data_unavailable with 0 items within the 7-day lookback window (polygon_news returned 0 rows), yet 20 items were dropped post-cutoff, indicating news activity existed but after the allowed_data_cutoff. This structural absence of pre-cutoff news corroboration is a critical signal: the price action is NOT anchored to a disclosed catalyst within the decision window. Wikipedia pageviews for 'Neang_Kakey' (the article title returned, unrelated to NAOV's business) show modest baseline activity (peak 18 views on 2025-03-11, 4 views on decision date 2025-03-17), providing no alternative-data support for a narrative catalyst. FMP sentiment shows 1 analyst buy rating as of 2024-11-01, with no recent upgrades or downgrades. The company's valuation metrics are deeply distressed (PE=-0.077, PB=0.034, net_margin=-583.74%), consistent with a loss-making micro-cap. The macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' (stress_score=2), which constrains equity appetite but does not explain a single-stock collapse. No SEC 8-K filing confirmation is available (sec_edgar adapter failed: cik_not_found). The absence of a concrete, disclosed catalyst—combined with the structural gap between the price action magnitude and available news/alternative data—suggests the move is either (1) driven by post-cutoff news not visible in this packet, (2) a technical/liquidity event (stock split aftermath, short squeeze unwind, or forced liquidation), or (3) a rumor-driven move without formal disclosure. The decision_time_discipline block confirms opening-window mode (first 10 minutes of OHLCV are cleanest read), and the price action is real and material. However, the NARRATIVE catalyst—the company's claim or disclosed reason for the move—is absent from the evidence packet.
invalidation_conditions: If a post-cutoff 8-K or press release disclosed a material adverse event (bankruptcy, delisting notice, major customer loss, regulatory action), the absence of pre-cutoff news would be explained and the catalyst would be 'real' but not captured in this packet.

If the stock split (1:11 on 2025-03-14) was accompanied by a reverse-split announcement of a capital raise or debt restructuring, that would be a concrete catalyst; no such disclosure is confirmed in filing_confirmation.

If retail/meme attention spiked post-cutoff (e.g., WSB or Reddit), the move could be momentum-driven; alternative_data_features.retail_meme_attention is not_evaluated, so this cannot be ruled in or out.
If insider selling or short-seller report emerged post-cutoff, that would explain the move; sec_form4 adapter failed (cik_not_found), so insider activity cannot be verified.
risk_flags: CRITICAL: No news catalyst within decision window. 20 items dropped post-cutoff suggests news broke AFTER 09:30 ET on 2025-03-17, making this a look-ahead risk if those items are later cited as the 'reason' for the move.

CRITICAL: Stock split (1:11) on 2025-03-14 creates mechanical price discontinuity. The 43.91% 5-day decline is partially attributable to split adjustment, but the magnitude and volume surge suggest additional selling pressure unrelated to the split itself.
HIGH: Micro-cap distressed valuation (net_margin=-583.74%, PE negative, PB=0.034) indicates a company in severe financial distress. The move may reflect deteriorating fundamentals not yet disclosed.

HIGH: Wikipedia pageviews for 'Neang_Kakey' (unrelated to NAOV's business) provide no corroboration of a narrative catalyst. The alternative_data_features block is largely not_evaluated or not_connected.
MEDIUM: Analyst sentiment is stale (last update 2024-11-01, 4+ months prior). No recent analyst action to support or contradict the move.
MEDIUM: SEC filing confirmation failed (cik_not_found). Cannot verify if an 8-K or other disclosure exists.
MEDIUM: Macro regime is restrictive (equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation_caution), which may suppress demand for distressed micro-caps, but does not explain a single-stock collapse.
LOW: Information integrity assessment is not_evaluated (placeholder in v1). Pollution-defense layer not implemented; cannot assess rumor risk.
suggested_improvements: Implement post-cutoff news adapter with explicit 'news_broke_after_cutoff' flag to disambiguate whether the catalyst is real but post-decision or absent entirely.
Wire sec_edgar CIK lookup for OTC/micro-cap tickers (NAOV is NASDAQ-listed but adapter failed; verify CIK mapping).
Add stock-split adjustment logic to price_snapshot to isolate split-driven vs. fundamental price changes.
Implement retail/meme attention adapter (reddit, stocktwits, fintwit) to detect momentum-driven moves in micro-caps.
Add insider-activity adapter (Form 4 filings) to detect insider selling or buying pressure.
Implement post-cutoff news summary (with explicit cutoff-violation flag) for PM review, rather than silently dropping 20 items.
Add sector-specific catalyst taxonomy for micro-cap biotech/pharma (NAOV appears to be a shell or distressed biotech); current taxonomy is generic.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure is sound, but the v1 generator's handling of post-cutoff news is a critical gap. The news_event_summary block correctly flags that 20 items were dropped, but does not provide a summary of those items' titles, timestamps, or content. For a surge-short candidate, this is precisely the information needed to assess whether the catalyst is real (disclosed post-cutoff) or absent (rumor-driven). The alternative_data_features block is largely a placeholder (retail_meme_attention, valuation_context, opencli_evidence all not_evaluated), which limits the agent's ability to detect momentum or rumor-driven moves in micro-caps. The information_integrity_assessment block is also a placeholder (pollution-defense layer not implemented), so the agent cannot assess coordinated-campaign or bot-like-pattern risk. These gaps are acknowledged in the packet's data_quality_flags and agent_ready_notes, but they materially constrain the agent's ability to classify the catalyst with high confidence.
recommended_followups: PM should manually review the 20 post-cutoff news items (via FMP or Polygon API directly) to determine if a material catalyst was disclosed after 09:30 ET on 2025-03-17.
Verify NAOV's CIK and SEC filing status; if the company is a shell or delisted, that would explain the absence of SEC filings and the distressed valuation.
Check if the 1:11 stock split on 2025-03-14 was accompanied by a reverse-split announcement or capital restructuring; if so, that is the catalyst.
Review insider trading activity (Form 4 filings) for the week of 2025-03-10 to 2025-03-17 to detect insider selling or buying pressure.
Check retail/meme platforms (Reddit WSB, Stocktwits, Fintwit) for NAOV mentions or sentiment spikes around 2025-03-14 to 2025-03-17.

If no post-cutoff news or insider activity is found, classify the move as 'technical/liquidity-driven' (stock split aftermath, short squeeze unwind, or forced liquidation) rather than 'catalyst-driven'.
For future NAOV decisions, implement a post-cutoff news summary (with explicit cutoff-violation flag) to allow PM to assess whether the catalyst is real but post-decision.
narrative_event.company_claim: No company claim or disclosed catalyst is present in the evidence packet. A 1:11 stock split occurred on 2025-03-14, which mechanically explains the price discontinuity but not the magnitude of the selling pressure. 20 news items were dropped post-cutoff, suggesting a catalyst may have been disclosed after 09:30 ET on 2025-03-17, but this is not visible in the packet.

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: NAOV exhibits a severe 5-day price collapse (-43.9%) following a 1:11 reverse split on 2025-03-14. Alternative-data corroboration is sparse and sector-mismatched. Wikipedia pageviews show modest baseline attention (1–18 views/day over 30 days, peak 18 on 2025-03-11) with no spike pattern consistent with a major catalyst. No news items exist within the cutoff window (polygon_news returned 0 rows). SEC filing adapters failed due to missing CIK (sec_edgar, sec_form4) or plan restrictions (sec_13f). GitHub adapter returned 0 rows (unmapped_ticker). Analyst sentiment is stale (last update 2024-11-01: 1 Buy, 0 Hold/Sell) and descriptor-only. Valuation metrics are distorted by the reverse split and negative profitability (PE=-0.077, net_margin=-583.74%, ROE=-0.392). The macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' with strict equity discipline. The structural absence of corroborating alt-data (wired adapters returning 0 rows for SEC filings, GitHub, news) relative to what a legitimate micro-cap equity should produce is itself bear-thesis evidence for a surge-short candidate. The narrative of a sharp price collapse is supported by price data, but the underlying business fundamentals and catalyst remain unverified. The absence of hard-to-fake signals (SEC filings, insider activity, developer footprint, recent news) is informative: either the company is genuinely opaque/dormant, or the surge-short thesis rests on price action alone without operational corroboration.

Reversal of the 1:11 split or announcement of a reverse-merger / acquisition that invalidates the bear thesis
Sudden spike in GitHub activity or developer hiring announcements indicating operational restart
Institutional ownership disclosure showing significant new positions by reputable funds

alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: NAOV's 5-day price collapse (-43.9%) is extreme and suggests either a major negative catalyst or technical forced selling. However, alternative-data sources that should corroborate a fundamental deterioration (SEC filings, insider selling, news coverage, analyst downgrades) are either absent (0 rows returned by wired adapters) or stale (analyst ratings last updated 2024-11-01). The Wikipedia pageviews show no spike pattern consistent with a major news event. The reverse split on 2025-03-14 (3 days before decision) is a structural event that may explain the price action mechanically, but does not confirm a fundamental bear thesis. surge_short.reasoning_summary: NAOV surged ~46.6% on 2025-03-17 opening (38.7 vs 26.4 prior close) but has already reversed -43.9% over 5 days. The surge coincides with a 1:11 reverse split on 2025-03-14, which mechanically inflates the opening price but does not represent value creation. Critical structural absences: (1) No news items in the 7-day window despite 20 items dropped post-cutoff, suggesting a narrative event occurred but is not verifiable in the packet; (2) SEC filing confirmation failed (cik_not_found for NAOV), preventing verification of any official disclosure; (3) Fundamental data is insufficient (no PIT-safe quarterly statements); (4) Valuation metrics are deeply distressed (negative PE, negative net margin -583.74%, PB=0.034); (5) Alternative data adapters failed or returned no rows (GitHub unmapped, Form 4 cik_not_found, 13F unavailable); (6) Analyst sentiment is stale (last rating Nov 2024, 1 buy). The company's actual sector/business model is not disclosed in the packet, preventing sector-specific evidence assessment. The absence of SEC filings for a listed equity claiming a material event is itself a bear-thesis signal under the universal-soft-veto principle: a legitimate listed company with a real catalyst should have a verifiable SEC footprint. The reverse split alone does not justify the surge; the subsequent -43.9% reversal suggests momentum rather than value creation. Macro regime is restrictive (equity_discipline=strict_quality_and_valuation_caution), which argues against holding a distressed, unverified micro-cap.
invalidation_conditions: If SEC 8-K or press release confirming a material acquisition, partnership, or product launch is later retrieved and verified, the structural-absence bear thesis weakens.
If quarterly fundamentals show positive revenue growth and path to profitability, the distressed valuation may be justified as a turnaround.
If institutional ownership data (13F) shows significant new positions by reputable funds, the absence of corroboration may be a data lag rather than a signal.
If the reverse split is part of a legitimate recapitalization or uplisting plan with disclosed milestones, the momentum may reflect real value creation.

surge_short.audit_rationale: NAOV surged 46.6% on 2025-03-17 opening but reversed -43.9% over 5 days. Surge coincides with 1:11 reverse split (mechanical, not value-creating).
